import { render } from '@testing-library/react-native';
import AirlineMilesMarketplace from '../../../src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace';
import { useAirlineMiles } from '../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram';
import ApiError from '../../../src/atomic/organisms/ApiError';
import AnimationArrows from '../../../src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows';
import { TestProvider } from '../../__mocks__/utils/test-provider';

let focusEffectCallback: (() => void) | null = null;

jest.mock('@react-navigation/native', () => ({
  useNavigation: jest.fn(),
  useRoute: jest.fn(),
  useNavigationState: jest.fn(() => 'AirlineMilesMarketplace'),
  useFocusEffect: jest.fn((callback) => {
    focusEffectCallback = callback;
  }),
  useIsFocused: jest.fn(() => true),
}));

jest.mock('../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram', () => ({
  useAirlineMiles: jest.fn(),
}));

jest.mock('../../../src/hooks/tracking', () => ({
  useScreenViewTracking: jest.fn(),
}));

jest.mock('react-native-webview', () => {
  const { View } = require('react-native');
  return {
    WebView: (props: any) => <View testID="webview" {...props} />,
  };
});

jest.mock('react-native', () => {
  const RN = jest.requireActual('react-native');
  return {
    ...RN,
    useWindowDimensions: () => ({ width: 375, height: 812 }),
    BackHandler: {
      addEventListener: jest.fn(() => ({ remove: jest.fn() })),
    },
  };
});

describe('AirlineMilesMarketplace', () => {
  const mockGoBack = jest.fn();
  const mockNavigate = jest.fn();
  const mockFetchToken = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(true);
  const mockReset = jest.fn();

  const mockNavigation = {
    goBack: mockGoBack,
    navigate: mockNavigate,
  } as any;

  const mockRoute = {
    key: 'AirlineMilesMarketplace',
    name: 'AirlineMilesMarketplace' as const,
    params: {},
  };

  const defaultProps = {
    navigation: mockNavigation,
    route: mockRoute,
  };

  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();
    focusEffectCallback = null;
  });

  it('should render AnimationArrows when loading', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: true,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(AnimationArrows)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render ApiError when there is an error', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: true,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render ApiError when redirectURL is empty', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render WebView when redirectURL is available', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: 'https://example.com/marketplace',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { getByTestId, getByText } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(getByTestId('webview')).toBeTruthy();
    expect(getByText('Milhas Aéreas')).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should register useFocusEffect callback', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: 'https://example.com',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(focusEffectCallback).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('should pass correct uri to WebView', () => {
    const testUrl = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc123';
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: testUrl,
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { getByTestId } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    const webView = getByTestId('webview');
    expect(webView.props.source).toEqual({ uri: testUrl });
  });
});

import { api } from '@semparar/mf-app-user';

jest.mock('@semparar/mf-app-user', () => ({
  api: {
    get: jest.fn(),
  },
}));

describe('AirlineMilesApi', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();
  });

  describe('getToken', () => {
    it('should call correct endpoint with params', async () => {
      const mockResponse = {
        data: {
          status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
          dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
        },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(api.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        '/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=MILEAGE'
      );
    });

    it('should return response data', async () => {
      const mockData = {
        status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
        dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({ data: mockData });

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      const result = await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(result).toEqual(mockData);
    });

    it('should handle API errors', async () => {
      const mockError = new Error('Network Error');
      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(mockError);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };

      await expect(airlineMilesApi.getToken(params)).rejects.toThrow('Network Error');
    });
  });
});

describe('AirlineMilesSlice', () => {
  let slice: AirlineMilesSlice;
  let mockSet: jest.Mock;

  beforeEach(() => {
    mockSet = jest.fn();
    slice = createAirlineMilesSlice(mockSet, jest.fn(), {} as any);
  });

  describe('initial state', () => {
    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe('');
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
    });
  });

  describe('actions', () => {
    it('should set loading state', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(true);
    });

    it('should set redirectURL', () => {
      const url = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc';
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setRedirectURL(url);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe(url);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
    });

    it('should set error', () => {
      const errorMessage = 'Failed to load';
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setError(errorMessage);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe(errorMessage);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(true);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });

    it('should clear error', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.clearError();

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...slice.airlineMiles,
          isError: true,
          errorMessage: 'some error',
        },
      });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
    });

    it('should reset state', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.reset();

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
        airlineMiles: {
          redirectURL: '',
          isLoading: false,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      });
    });
  });
});

  },
}));

jest.mock('zustand/shallow', () => ({
  useShallow: (selector: any) => selector,
}));

describe('useAirlineMiles', () => {
  const mockSetLoading = jest.fn();
  const mockSetRedirectURL = jest.fn();
  const mockSetError = jest.fn();
  const mockReset = jest.fn();

  const mockActions = {
    setLoading: mockSetLoading,
    setRedirectURL: mockSetRedirectURL,
    setError: mockSetError,
    reset: mockReset,
  };

  const mockState = {
    redirectURL: '',
    isLoading: false,
    isError: false,
    errorMessage: '',
  };

  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();

    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((selector: any) => {
      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
        return selector({ airlineMiles: mockState });
      }
      return mockState;
    });

    (useRootStore.getState as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      airlineMilesActions: mockActions,
    });
  });

  it('should return initial state', () => {
    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    expect(result.current.redirectURL).toBe('');
    expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    expect(result.current.isError).toBe(false);
  });

  it('should fetch token successfully', async () => {
    const mockResponse = {
      status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
      dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
    };

    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = false;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(true);
    expect(mockSetLoading).toHaveBeenCalledWith(true);
    expect(mockSetRedirectURL).toHaveBeenCalledWith(mockResponse.dados.redirectURL);
  });

  it('should handle API error response', async () => {
    const mockResponse = {
      status: { codigoRetorno: 1, mensagem: 'Token expired' },
      dados: { redirectURL: '' },
    };

    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Token expired');
  });

  it('should handle network error', async () => {
    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(new Error('Network error'));

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Failed to load airline miles marketplace');
  });

  it('should call reset', () => {
    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    act(() => {
      result.current.reset();
    });

    expect(mockReset).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('should have stable function references', () => {
    const { result, rerender } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    const initialFetch = result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken;
    const initialReset = result.current.reset;

    rerender({});

    expect(result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken).toBe(initialFetch);
    expect(result.current.reset).toBe(initialReset);
  });
});

import { AirlineMilesSlice, createAirlineMilesSlice } from "../services/airlineMiles/store";
import { AdditionalCardSlice, createAdditionalCardSlice } from '../../services/additionalCard';
import { createParameterPointsProgramSlice, ParameterPointsProgramSlice, createPermissionSlice, PermissionSlice } from './rewardProgram';

export type CardPlaceRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  AdditionalCardSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice;

export type Slice<T> = StateCreator<CardPlaceRootStore, [], [], T>;
export const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<CardPlaceRootStore, [], [], T>) => slice as Slice<T>;

export function setSlicesCardPlace<T extends CardPlaceRootStore>(...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>) {
  return {
    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AdditionalCardSlice>(createAdditionalCardSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
  };

"}]},"uuid":"3f29f3ed-5359-4bf8-a108-e74837419f1b","timestamp":"2026-01-14T00:07:13.591Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/store/airlineMiles.store.ts","content":"import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
import type { AirlineMilesActions, AirlineMilesState } from '../models';

export type AirlineMilesSlice = {
  airlineMiles: AirlineMilesState;
  airlineMilesActions: AirlineMilesActions;
};

const initialAirlineMilesState: AirlineMilesState = {
  redirectURL: '',
  isLoading: false,
  isError: false,
  errorMessage: '',
};

export const createAirlineMilesSlice: StateCreator<
  AirlineMilesSlice,
  [],
  [],
  AirlineMilesSlice
> = (set) => ({
  airlineMiles: initialAirlineMilesState,

  airlineMilesActions: {
    setRedirectURL: (url) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          redirectURL: url,
          isLoading: false,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      })),

    setLoading: (isLoading) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isLoading,
          ...(isLoading && {
            isError: false,
            errorMessage: '',
          }),
        },
      })),

    setError: (error) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isLoading: false,
          isError: true,
          errorMessage: error,
        },
      })),

    clearError: () =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      })),

    reset: () =>
      set({
        airlineMiles: initialAirlineMilesState,
      }),
  },
});

"}]},"uuid":"a84183d8-0533-46ed-8a54-6eddd38cddec","timestamp":"2026-01-14T00:07:13.591Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts","content":"import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { airlineMilesService } from '../services';
import { AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS } from '../models';

export const useAirlineMiles = () => {
  const airlineMiles = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.airlineMiles));

  const fetchAirlineMilesToken = useCallback(async (): Promise<boolean> => {
    const { airlineMilesActions } = useRootStore.getState();

    try {
      airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);

      const response = await airlineMilesService.getToken(AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS);

      if (response.status.codigoRetorno === 0) {
        airlineMilesActions.setRedirectURL(response.dados.redirectURL);
        return true;
      } else {
        airlineMilesActions.setError(response.status.mensagem || 'Failed to get token');
        return false;
      }
    } catch (error) {
      airlineMilesActions.setError('Failed to load airline miles marketplace');
      return false;
    }
  }, []);

  const reset = useCallback(() => {
    useRootStore.getState().airlineMilesActions.reset();
  }, []);

  return {
    ...airlineMiles,
    fetchAirlineMilesToken,
    reset,
  };
};

import type { AirlineMilesTokenParams, AirlineMilesTokenResponse } from '../models';

export class AirlineMilesService {
  private readonly repository: AirlineMilesRepository;

  constructor() {
    this.repository = new AirlineMilesRepository();
  }

  async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
    return this.repository.getToken(params);
  }
}

export const airlineMilesService = new AirlineMilesService();

{"parentUuid":"cc60d0ee-cf8a-4a1f-a01a-16c9c0d710ad","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place","sessionId":"5735e6b2-f8e3-4c50-adec-fd2c4a23ba61","version":"2.1.6","gitBranch":"feature/milhaswebview--newarch","agentId":"a7937d1","slug":"wild-watching-riddle","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PyZrkc9cUoDv5pw4tELdqa","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→import type { AirlineMilesActions, AirlineMilesState } from '../models';
     3→
     4→export type AirlineMilesSlice = {
     5→  airlineMiles: AirlineMilesState;
     6→  airlineMilesActions: AirlineMilesActions;
     7→};
     8→
     9→const initialAirlineMilesState: AirlineMilesState = {
    10→  redirectURL: '',
    11→  isLoading: false,
    12→  isError: false,
    13→  errorMessage: '',
    14→};
    15→
    16→export const createAirlineMilesSlice: StateCreator<
    17→  AirlineMilesSlice,
    18→  [],
    19→  [],
    20→  AirlineMilesSlice
    21→> = (set) => ({
    22→  airlineMiles: initialAirlineMilesState,
    23→
    24→  airlineMilesActions: {
    25→    setRedirectURL: (url) =>
    26→      set((state) => ({
    27→        airlineMiles: {
    28→          ...state.airlineMiles,
    29→          redirectURL: url,
    30→          isLoading: false,
    31→          isError: false,
    32→          errorMessage: '',
    33→        },
    34→      })),
    35→
    36→    setLoading: (isLoading) =>
    37→      set((state) => ({
    38→        airlineMiles: {
    39→          ...state.airlineMiles,
    40→          isLoading,

"}]},"uuid":"234ace53-33dd-402b-bf79-47a20602817e","timestamp":"2026-01-14T00:07:35.389Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/store/airlineMiles.store.ts","content":"import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
import type { AirlineMilesActions, AirlineMilesState } from '../models';

export type AirlineMilesSlice = {
  airlineMiles: AirlineMilesState;
  airlineMilesActions: AirlineMilesActions;
};

const initialAirlineMilesState: AirlineMilesState = {
  redirectURL: '',
  isLoading: false,
  isError: false,
  errorMessage: '',
};

export const createAirlineMilesSlice: StateCreator<
  AirlineMilesSlice,
  [],
  [],
  AirlineMilesSlice
> = (set) => ({
  airlineMiles: initialAirlineMilesState,

  airlineMilesActions: {
    setRedirectURL: (url) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          redirectURL: url,
          isLoading: false,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      })),

    setLoading: (isLoading) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isLoading,
          ...(isLoading && {
            isError: false,
            errorMessage: '',
          }),
        },
      })),

    setError: (error) =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isLoading: false,
          isError: true,
          errorMessage: error,
        },
      })),

    clearError: () =>
      set((state) => ({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...state.airlineMiles,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      })),

    reset: () =>
      set({
        airlineMiles: initialAirlineMilesState,
      }),
  },
});

describe('AirlineMilesSlice', () => {
  let slice: AirlineMilesSlice;
  let mockSet: jest.Mock;

  beforeEach(() => {
    mockSet = jest.fn();
    slice = createAirlineMilesSlice(mockSet, jest.fn(), {} as any);
  });

  describe('initial state', () => {
    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe('');
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
    });
  });

  describe('actions', () => {
    it('should set loading state', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(true);
    });

    it('should set redirectURL', () => {
      const url = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc';

import { AirlineMilesSlice, createAirlineMilesSlice } from "../services/airlineMiles/store";
import { AdditionalCardSlice, createAdditionalCardSlice } from '../../services/additionalCard';
import { createParameterPointsProgramSlice, ParameterPointsProgramSlice, createPermissionSlice, PermissionSlice } from './rewardProgram';

export type CardPlaceRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  AdditionalCardSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice;

export type Slice<T> = StateCreator<CardPlaceRootStore, [], [], T>;
export const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<CardPlaceRootStore, [], [], T>) => slice as Slice<T>;

export function setSlicesCardPlace<T extends CardPlaceRootStore>(...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>) {
  return {
    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AdditionalCardSlice>(createAdditionalCardSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
  };
}

import { api } from '@semparar/mf-app-user';

jest.mock('@semparar/mf-app-user', () => ({
  api: {
    get: jest.fn(),
  },
}));

describe('AirlineMilesApi', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();
  });

  describe('getToken', () => {
    it('should call correct endpoint with params', async () => {
      const mockResponse = {
        data: {
          status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
          dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
        },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(api.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        '/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=MILEAGE'
      );
    });

    it('should return response data', async () => {
      const mockData = {
        status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
        dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({ data: mockData });

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      const result = await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(result).toEqual(mockData);
    });

    it('should handle API errors', async () => {
      const mockError = new Error('Network Error');
      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(mockError);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };

      await expect(airlineMilesApi.getToken(params)).rejects.toThrow('Network Error');
    });
  });
});

import { useAirlineMiles } from '../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram';
import { useRootStore } from '../../../src/store';
import { airlineMilesService } from '../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services';

jest.mock('../../../src/store', () => ({
  useRootStore: Object.assign(
    jest.fn(),
    {
      getState: jest.fn(),
    }
  ),
}));

jest.mock('../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services', () => ({
  airlineMilesService: {
    getToken: jest.fn(),
  },
}));

jest.mock('zustand/shallow', () => ({
  useShallow: (selector: any) => selector,
}));

describe('useAirlineMiles', () => {
  const mockSetLoading = jest.fn();
  const mockSetRedirectURL = jest.fn();
  const mockSetError = jest.fn();
  const mockReset = jest.fn();

  const mockActions = {
    setLoading: mockSetLoading,
    setRedirectURL: mockSetRedirectURL,
    setError: mockSetError,
    reset: mockReset,
  };

  const mockState = {
    redirectURL: '',
    isLoading: false,
    isError: false,
    errorMessage: '',
  };

  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();

    (useRootStore as unknown as jest.Mock).mockImplementation((selector: any) => {
      if (typeof selector === 'function') {
        return selector({ airlineMiles: mockState });
      }
      return mockState;
    });

    (useRootStore.getState as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      airlineMilesActions: mockActions,
    });
  });

  it('should return initial state', () => {
    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    expect(result.current.redirectURL).toBe('');
    expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    expect(result.current.isError).toBe(false);
  });

  it('should fetch token successfully', async () => {
    const mockResponse = {
      status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
      dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
    };

    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = false;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(true);
    expect(mockSetLoading).toHaveBeenCalledWith(true);
    expect(mockSetRedirectURL).toHaveBeenCalledWith(mockResponse.dados.redirectURL);
  });

  it('should handle API error response', async () => {
    const mockResponse = {
      status: { codigoRetorno: 1, mensagem: 'Token expired' },
      dados: { redirectURL: '' },
    };

    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Token expired');
  });

  it('should handle network error', async () => {
    (airlineMilesService.getToken as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(new Error('Network error'));

    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    let fetchResult: boolean = true;
    await act(async () => {
      fetchResult = await result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken();
    });

    expect(fetchResult).toBe(false);
    expect(mockSetError).toHaveBeenCalledWith('Failed to load airline miles marketplace');
  });

  it('should call reset', () => {
    const { result } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    act(() => {
      result.current.reset();
    });

    expect(mockReset).toHaveBeenCalled();
  });

  it('should have stable function references', () => {
    const { result, rerender } = renderHook(() => useAirlineMiles());

    const initialFetch = result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken;
    const initialReset = result.current.reset;

    rerender({});

    expect(result.current.fetchAirlineMilesToken).toBe(initialFetch);
    expect(result.current.reset).toBe(initialReset);
  });
});

import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { airlineMilesService } from '../services';
import { AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS } from '../models';

export const useAirlineMiles = () => {
  const airlineMiles = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.airlineMiles));

  const fetchAirlineMilesToken = useCallback(async (): Promise<boolean> => {
    const { airlineMilesActions } = useRootStore.getState();

    try {
      airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);

      const response = await airlineMilesService.getToken(AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS);

      if (response.status.codigoRetorno === 0) {
        airlineMilesActions.setRedirectURL(response.dados.redirectURL);
        return true;
      } else {
        airlineMilesActions.setError(response.status.mensagem || 'Failed to get token');
        return false;
      }
    } catch (error) {
      airlineMilesActions.setError('Failed to load airline miles marketplace');
      return false;
    }
  }, []);

  const reset = useCallback(() => {
    useRootStore.getState().airlineMilesActions.reset();
  }, []);

  return {
    ...airlineMiles,
    fetchAirlineMilesToken,
    reset,
  };
};

import type { AirlineMilesTokenParams, AirlineMilesTokenResponse } from '../models';

export class AirlineMilesApi {
  async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
    const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(params);
    const response = await api.get<AirlineMilesTokenResponse>(
      `/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?${urlParams.toString()}`
    );
    return response.data;
  }
}

export const airlineMilesApi = new AirlineMilesApi();

import { render } from '@testing-library/react-native';
import AirlineMilesMarketplace from '../../../src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace';
import { useAirlineMiles } from '../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram';
import ApiError from '../../../src/atomic/organisms/ApiError';
import AnimationArrows from '../../../src/atomic/atoms/AnimationArrows';
import { TestProvider } from '../../__mocks__/utils/test-provider';

let focusEffectCallback: (() => void) | null = null;

jest.mock('@react-navigation/native', () => ({
  useNavigation: jest.fn(),
  useRoute: jest.fn(),
  useNavigationState: jest.fn(() => 'AirlineMilesMarketplace'),
  useFocusEffect: jest.fn((callback) => {
    focusEffectCallback = callback;
  }),
  useIsFocused: jest.fn(() => true),
}));

jest.mock('../../../src/newArch/store/rewardProgram', () => ({
  useAirlineMiles: jest.fn(),
}));

jest.mock('../../../src/hooks/tracking', () => ({
  useScreenViewTracking: jest.fn(),
}));

jest.mock('react-native-webview', () => {
  const { View } = require('react-native');
  return {
    WebView: (props: any) => <View testID="webview" {...props} />,
  };
});

jest.mock('react-native', () => {
  const RN = jest.requireActual('react-native');
  return {
    ...RN,
    useWindowDimensions: () => ({ width: 375, height: 812 }),
    BackHandler: {
      addEventListener: jest.fn(() => ({ remove: jest.fn() })),
    },
  };
});

describe('AirlineMilesMarketplace', () => {
  const mockGoBack = jest.fn();
  const mockNavigate = jest.fn();
  const mockFetchToken = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(true);
  const mockReset = jest.fn();

  const mockNavigation = {
    goBack: mockGoBack,
    navigate: mockNavigate,
  } as any;

  const mockRoute = {
    key: 'AirlineMilesMarketplace',
    name: 'AirlineMilesMarketplace' as const,
    params: {},
  };

  const defaultProps = {
    navigation: mockNavigation,
    route: mockRoute,
  };

  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();
    focusEffectCallback = null;
  });

  it('should render AnimationArrows when loading', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: true,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(AnimationArrows)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render ApiError when there is an error', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: true,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render ApiError when redirectURL is empty', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: '',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { UNSAFE_getByType } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(UNSAFE_getByType(ApiError)).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should render WebView when redirectURL is available', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: 'https://example.com/marketplace',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { getByTestId, getByText } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(getByTestId('webview')).toBeTruthy();
    expect(getByText('Milhas Aéreas')).toBeTruthy();
  });

  it('should register useFocusEffect callback', () => {
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: 'https://example.com',
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    expect(focusEffectCallback).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('should pass correct uri to WebView', () => {
    const testUrl = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc123';
    (useAirlineMiles as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
      redirectURL: testUrl,
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
      fetchAirlineMilesToken: mockFetchToken,
      reset: mockReset,
    });

    const { getByTestId } = render(
      <TestProvider>
        <AirlineMilesMarketplace {...defaultProps} />
      </TestProvider>
    );

    const webView = getByTestId('webview');
    expect(webView.props.source).toEqual({ uri: testUrl });
  });
});

import { api } from '@semparar/mf-app-user';

jest.mock('@semparar/mf-app-user', () => ({
  api: {
    get: jest.fn(),
  },
}));

describe('AirlineMilesApi', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    jest.clearAllMocks();
  });

  describe('getToken', () => {
    it('should call correct endpoint with params', async () => {
      const mockResponse = {
        data: {
          status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
          dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
        },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(mockResponse);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(api.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        '/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=MILEAGE'
      );
    });

    it('should return response data', async () => {
      const mockData = {
        status: { codigoRetorno: 0, mensagem: 'Success' },
        dados: { redirectURL: 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc' },
      };

      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({ data: mockData });

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };
      const result = await airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);

      expect(result).toEqual(mockData);
    });

    it('should handle API errors', async () => {
      const mockError = new Error('Network Error');
      (api.get as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(mockError);

      const params = { redirectTo: 'MARKETPLACE', catalog: 'MILEAGE' };

      await expect(airlineMilesApi.getToken(params)).rejects.toThrow('Network Error');
    });
  });
});

describe('AirlineMilesSlice', () => {
  let slice: AirlineMilesSlice;
  let mockSet: jest.Mock;

  beforeEach(() => {
    mockSet = jest.fn();
    slice = createAirlineMilesSlice(mockSet, jest.fn(), {} as any);
  });

  describe('initial state', () => {
    it('should have correct initial values', () => {
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe('');
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
      expect(slice.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
    });
  });

  describe('actions', () => {
    it('should set loading state', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setLoading(true);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(true);
    });

    it('should set redirectURL', () => {
      const url = 'https://vertem.com/marketplace?token=abc';
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setRedirectURL(url);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.redirectURL).toBe(url);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
    });

    it('should set error', () => {
      const errorMessage = 'Failed to load';
      slice.airlineMilesActions.setError(errorMessage);

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({ airlineMiles: slice.airlineMiles });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe(errorMessage);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(true);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });

    it('should clear error', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.clearError();

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function));

      const updateFn = mockSet.mock.calls[0][0];
      const newState = updateFn({
        airlineMiles: {
          ...slice.airlineMiles,
          isError: true,
          errorMessage: 'some error',
        },
      });

      expect(newState.airlineMiles.isError).toBe(false);
      expect(newState.airlineMiles.errorMessage).toBe('');
    });

    it('should reset state', () => {
      slice.airlineMilesActions.reset();

      expect(mockSet).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
        airlineMiles: {
          redirectURL: '',
          isLoading: false,
          isError: false,
          errorMessage: '',
        },
      });
    });
  });
});

content.content:
# Anthropic Agent Harness System Overview

## 1. System Purpose

The Anthropic Agent Harness enables long-running AI agents to work effectively across multiple context windows. As described in the research, "agents must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before." The system solves this through specialized initialization and structured progress tracking.

## 2. Core Architecture

The harness implements a **two-agent approach**:

- **Initializer Agent**: Runs once on first execution to establish the foundational environment
- **Coding Agent**: Operates in subsequent sessions, making incremental progress while maintaining clarity

## 3. claude-progress.txt Purpose and Structure

This file serves as a **session memory bridge**. It maintains:
- A chronological log of completed work by previous agent instances
- Summaries of implemented features and their status
- Clear documentation enabling rapid context recovery

The file helps agents "quickly understand the state of work when starting with a fresh context window."

## 4. feature_list.json Purpose and Structure

A comprehensive **feature inventory** structured as JSON containing:
- Individual feature descriptions with step-by-step validation criteria
- Status flags (marked "passes": true/false)
- Categorical organization of functional requirements

Example structure:
```json
{
  "category": "functional",
  "description": "Feature description",
  "steps": ["Step 1", "Step 2"],
  "passes": false
}
```

The model can only modify the `passes` field to prevent accidental deletions or modifications.

## 5. Implementation Files

The initializer creates:
- **init.sh**: Automated development server startup script
- **claude-progress.txt**: Progress documentation
- **feature_list.json**: Structured feature requirements
- Initial git repository with baseline commit

## 6. Workflow and Best Practices

**Session Startup Sequence**:
1. Read current directory location
2. Review git logs and progress files
3. Select highest-priority incomplete feature
4. Start development server via init.sh
5. Run end-to-end verification tests

**Development Guidelines**:
- Work on one feature per session only
- Make git commits with descriptive messages after changes
- Use browser automation tools for comprehensive testing
- Update progress files before session conclusion
- Leave code in "production-ready" state (no half-implemented features)

**Testing Requirements**:
Agents should conduct end-to-end verification using appropriate tools (like Puppeteer for web apps) rather than relying solely on unit tests.

<result>Perfect! I've completed my comprehensive exploration. Let me compile my findings.

## COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT: airlineMiles Feature in newArch

### 1. FILE STRUCTURE & MAPPING

**AirlineMiles Feature Files:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/models/airlineMiles.model.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/api/airlineMiles.api.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services/airlineMiles.service.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/store/airlineMiles.store.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts`

**Test Files:**
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/api.test.ts`
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/store.test.ts`
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/useAirlineMiles.test.tsx`

---

### 2. INTERACTION FLOW & ARCHITECTURE

**Layered Architecture (Clean Architecture Pattern):**

```
API Layer (airlineMiles.api.ts)
  └─→ Makes HTTP calls via @semparar/mf-app-user's api client
  
Repository Layer (airlineMiles.repository.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesApi, proxies calls
  
Service Layer (airlineMiles.service.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesRepository, proxies calls
  
State Management (airlineMiles.store.ts)
  └─→ Zustand slice with actions for loading/error/redirect URL
  
Hook Layer (useAirlineMiles.ts)
  └─→ Orchestrates service calls and state updates via useRootStore
  
Screen Layer (AirlineMilesMarketplace/)
  └─→ Consumes useAirlineMiles hook, renders WebView
```

**Data Flow for Token Retrieval:**
1. Screen calls `useAirlineMiles()` hook
2. Hook calls `fetchAirlineMilesToken()` callback
3. Callback invokes `airlineMilesService.getToken(AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS)`
4. Service chain: Service → Repository → Api
5. Api makes GET to `/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=/mileage`
6. Response success (codigoRetorno === 0) triggers `setRedirectURL()`
7. Store updates state, screen renders WebViewScreen with URL

---

### 3. CODEBASE USAGE LOCATIONS

**Screen Implementation:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts` - Custom hook `useAirlineMilesMarketplace()`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace/view.tsx` - React component rendering WebView

**Hook Usage:**
- Imported and used in `AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts`
- Direct screen-specific logic wrapper around the store hook

---

### 4. PATTERNS & CONSISTENCY ANALYSIS

**Consistent Patterns:**
✓ Service/Repository/Api layering matches `permission` and `parameter` features
✓ Zustand slice pattern with state + actions object structure
✓ Hook uses `useShallow` from zustand for selector optimization
✓ useCallback with empty dependencies for stable function references
✓ Error handling with boolean return values

**Inconsistencies & Issues Detected:**

**CRITICAL ISSUE #1: Hooks Index Export Bug**
```typescript
// src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts
export * from './usePermission';
export * from './usePermission';  // ← DUPLICATE EXPORT!
// Missing: export * from './useAirlineMiles';
```
- `useAirlineMiles` is NOT exported from the hooks index
- Tests work because they import directly from the file
- Usage in `AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts` imports directly: `from '../../newArch/store/rewardProgram'` (works via rewardProgram/index.ts re-export)
- **This should be fixed to:** `export * from './useAirlineMiles';` and remove duplicate usePermission

**ISSUE #2: Store Cleanup Missing airlineMiles**
```typescript
// src/newArch/store/utils/cleanup.ts
export function cardPlaceStoreCleanup() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    additionalCardActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    // ← MISSING: airlineMilesActions
  } = useRootStore.getState();
  
  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  additionalCardActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  // ← MISSING: airlineMilesActions.reset();
}
```
- When store cleanup happens, airlineMiles state persists
- Could cause stale redirect URLs across different sessions

**ISSUE #3: Architectural Bypass - Thin Layers**
```typescript
// repository just wraps api
async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
  return this.api.getToken(params);  // Pure passthrough
}

// service just wraps repository
async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
  return this.repository.getToken(params);  // Pure passthrough
}
```
- No business logic in Repository or Service layers
- Contrast: `permission.service.ts` has actual logic (checkHasRedeemed, markAsRedeemed)
- For simplicity, could flatten to just Api → Hook, but inconsistent with permission pattern

**ISSUE #4: Parameter Feature Has NO Hook**
```typescript
// Only permission and airlineMiles have hooks in rewardProgram
// Parameter uses service functions directly imported in permission.service.ts
```
- Inconsistent: parameter uses functional service exports, while airlineMiles/permission use hooks
- Parameter pattern: `export async function getParameterPointsProgram(): Promise<void>`
- AirlineMiles pattern: `export const useAirlineMiles = () => {...}`

---

### 5. COMPARISON WITH OTHER FEATURES

| Aspect | AirlineMiles | Permission | Parameter |
|--------|--------------|-----------|-----------|
| **State Model** | Simple (4 fields) | Simple (2 fields) | Complex (3 fields + enum) |
| **Status Tracking** | Implicit (isLoading, isError) | Explicit enum (PermissionStatus) | Explicit enum (ParameterPointsProgramStatus) |
| **Service Pattern** | Passthrough layers | Business logic + state mutations | Business logic, no mutations |
| **Hook Pattern** | React hook returning state + functions | React hook with selector logic | No hook, direct function imports |
| **Test Coverage** | Api + Store + Hook | Service functions only tested implicitly | Service function likely tested indirectly |
| **Export Strategy** | Via rewardProgram/index.ts | Via hooks/index.ts (exported) | Via rewardProgram/index.ts |

---

### 6. STYLE GUIDE COMPLIANCE

**Follows:**
✓ camelCase naming throughout
✓ Zustand `StateCreator` type pattern
✓ Class-based Api/Repository/Service pattern
✓ Zustand `useShallow` optimization
✓ TypeScript strict typing
✓ JSDoc-free code (clean, self-explanatory)
✓ `export const instance = new Class()` singleton pattern

**Deviations:**
- AirlineMiles state model is simpler than Permission (lacks explicit Status enum)
- Repository/Service layers are passthrough (violates Single Responsibility Principle)
- Inconsistent with parameter feature's service function pattern

---

### 7. KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY

**Strengths:**
- Clear separation of concerns (Api → Repository → Service → Store → Hook)
- Strong typing with TypeScript
- Comprehensive test coverage (3 test files covering all layers)
- Proper use of Zustand patterns and React optimization hooks

**Critical Fixes Needed:**
1. **URGENT:** Add `useAirlineMiles` export to `src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts`
2. **HIGH:** Add `airlineMilesActions.reset()` to cleanup utility
3. **MEDIUM:** Consider adding explicit Status enum like Permission feature (IDLE, LOADING, SUCCESS, ERROR)
4. **MEDIUM:** Justify or flatten passthrough Repository/Service layers

**Test Status:**
- All tests are well-structured with proper mocking
- Tests verify: api calls, state mutations, hook behavior, error handling
- Tests use isolated component testing (renderHook, act patterns)</result>

### 4. **Slice Merging in Store Setup**

**Main entry point:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/slices.ts`

```typescript
export type CardPlaceRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  AdditionalCardSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice;

export function setSlicesCardPlace<T extends CardPlaceRootStore>(...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>) {
  return {
    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AdditionalCardSlice>(createAdditionalCardSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
  };
}
```

- **Service** (`airlineMiles.service.ts`): Business logic, orchestrates repository calls
- **Store/Slice** (`airlineMiles.store.ts`): Zustand slice with state and actions (setRedirectURL, setLoading, setError, clearError, reset)
- **Hook** (`useAirlineMiles.ts`): Consumer hook exposing state and fetchAirlineMilesToken callback
- **Screen** (`AirlineMilesMarketplace/`): Hook Component Pattern with index.ts (logic) and view.tsx (UI)

**Zustand Module Augmentation:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/@types/store.d.ts` declares useRootStore type

- **Jest setup:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/jest.setup.env.js`

- **Functions exposed:** Both selector pattern (for rendering) and getState() (for imperative actions)

<result>Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report of my findings:

## PERMISSION FEATURE ARCHITECTURE EXPLORATION

### 1. COMPLETE FILE STRUCTURE MAP

**Permission Feature Layers:**

```
src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/
├── models/
│   └── permission.model.ts
├── api/
│   └── permission.api.ts
├── repository/
│   └── permission.repository.ts
├── services/
│   └── permission.service.ts
├── store/
│   └── permission.store.ts
└── hooks/
    └── usePermission.ts
```

---

### 2. UI TO API DATA FLOW

**Complete Request/Response Flow:**

```
UI Layer (usePermission Hook)
    ↓
usePermission.ts
    ├─ getClientNRC() → Gets accountId from Redux
    ├─ useRootStore((state) => state.rewardsPermission) → Gets state
    └─ Returns: { hasRedeemed, isLoading, isError, checkHasRedeemed, markAsRedeemed }
    ↓
Service Layer (permission.service.ts)
    ├─ checkHasRedeemed(accountId: string): Promise<boolean>
    └─ markAsRedeemed(accountId: string): Promise<boolean>
    ↓
Repository Layer (permission.repository.ts)
    └─ mapPermissionResponse(response)
    ↓
API Layer (permission.api.ts)
    ├─ getPermissionApi({ accountNumber })
    │   GET /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/consult-permission
    │   Header: codigoCliente: accountNumber
    └─ savePermissionApi(document)
        POST /fintech/v1/rewardProgram/insert-permission
    ↓
Zustand Store (permission.store.ts)
    └─ State Management: { status, hasRedeemed }
```

---

### 3. FILE-BY-FILE ANALYSIS

**A. Models Layer** (`permission.model.ts`)
- **GetPermissionParams**: Request params with accountNumber
- **PermissionValue**: Nested object containing `invoiceCashbackRedemption.redeemedAtDate`
- **PermissionDocument**: Server document with codigoCliente, value, status(0|1)
- **PermissionDocumentInput**: Input variant (value is JSON string)
- **PermissionDocumentResponse**: API response wrapper
- **PermissionStatus enum**: IDLE, LOADING, SUCCESS, ERROR
- **PermissionState interface**: hasRedeemed (boolean|null), status

**B. API Layer** (`permission.api.ts`)
- **getPermissionApi()**: 
  - GET request to 'fintech/v1/rewardProgram/consult-permission'
  - Passes accountNumber as header (codigoCliente)
  - Returns AxiosResponse<PermissionDocumentResponse>
- **savePermissionApi()**: 
  - POST request to 'fintech/v1/rewardProgram/insert-permission'
  - Sends PermissionDocumentInput (status: 1, value: JSON string)
  - Returns AxiosResponse

**C. Repository Layer** (`permission.repository.ts`)
- **mapPermissionResponse()**: Single function
  - Extracts response?.data?.value
  - Returns PermissionValue | null

**D. Service Layer** (`permission.service.ts`)
- **checkHasRedeemed(accountId)**:
  1. Sets status to LOADING
  2. Calls getPermissionApi
  3. Checks HTTP status 200-299
  4. Extracts redeemedAtDate
  5. Sets hasRedeemed (Boolean(redeemedAt))
  6. Returns boolean
  7. Catches errors → sets ERROR status → returns false
  
- **markAsRedeemed(accountId)**:
  1. Constructs PermissionDocumentInput with:
     - codigoCliente: accountId
     - status: 1
     - value: JSON stringified object with redeemedAtDate = now
  2. Calls savePermissionApi
  3. Checks HTTP status 200-299
  4. Sets hasRedeemed(true) and status SUCCESS
  5. Returns boolean
  6. Catches errors → returns false (no status update)

**E. Store Layer** (`permission.store.ts`)
- **PermissionSlice Interface**:
  - rewardsPermission: PermissionState
  - rewardPermissionActions: PermissionActions
  
- **PermissionState Initial**:
  ```
  { status: IDLE, hasRedeemed: null }
  ```
  
- **PermissionActions**:
  - setStatus(status)
  - setHasRedeemed(hasRedeemed) → also sets status to SUCCESS
  - reset() → back to initial state

**F. Hook Layer** (`usePermission.ts`)
- **usePermission()**:
  - Gets accountId from getClientNRC() (Redux selector)
  - Selects state: { hasRedeemed, status }
  - Derives: isLoading, isError
  - Returns:
    ```
    {
      hasRedeemed: boolean | null,
      isLoading: boolean,
      isError: boolean,
      checkHasRedeemed: (forceRefresh?: boolean) => Promise<boolean>,
      markAsRedeemed: () => Promise<boolean>
    }
    ```
  - **checkHasRedeemed()**: 
    - Returns cached value unless forceRefresh=true
    - Throws if accountId undefined
  - **markAsRedeemed()**: 
    - Returns false if no accountId

---

### 4. EXISTING TEST PATTERNS (From airlineMiles tests)

**Test Structure Found:**

**Location**: `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/`

1. **API Tests** (`api.test.ts`):
   - Mock `@semparar/mf-app-user` api module
   - Test correct endpoint and params
   - Test response data extraction
   - Test error handling

2. **Store Tests** (`store.test.ts`):
   - Create slice with mock `set` function
   - Test initial state
   - Test each action by:
     - Calling action
     - Extracting updateFn from mockSet calls
     - Calling updateFn with state
     - Asserting new state
   - Test reset to initial state

3. **Hook Tests** (`useAirlineMiles.test.tsx`):
   - Mock dependencies: useRootStore, services, zustand/shallow
   - Mock store state and actions
   - Use renderHook + act() for async operations
   - Test initial state
   - Test success scenarios
   - Test error scenarios (API response + network)
   - Test function stability (memoization)

**Testing Library Pattern**:
```typescript
import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react-native';
```

---

### 5. TEST SETUP & MOCKS STRUCTURE

**Test Store** (`__tests__/__mocks__/test-store.ts`):
```typescript
- Uses zustand create() with devtools, persist, immer middleware
- Composes all slices via setSlicesCardPlace()
- Only for test environment
```

**Mock Utilities** (`__tests__/__mocks__/utils/`):
- `test-provider.tsx`: Redux + Theme provider for component tests
- `redux-mock-provider.tsx`: redux-mock-store wrapper
- `navigation.ts`: Navigation mocks

**Jest Config** (`jest.config.js`):
- Preset: react-native
- Transform: babel-jest + ts-jest
- Module mapper: `~` alias to `src/`
- Mock for @semparar/mf-app-user at `__tests__/@semparar/mf-app-user`
- Test environment: node
- Coverage directory: `__tests__/coverage`

---

### 6. WHAT NEEDS TESTING FOR PERMISSION FEATURE

**Critical Test Coverage Gaps:**

1. **API Layer Tests** (permission.api.ts):
   - ✗ getPermissionApi with valid params
   - ✗ getPermissionApi error handling
   - ✗ savePermissionApi with valid document
   - ✗ savePermissionApi error handling
   - ✗ Correct header/body formatting

2. **Repository Layer Tests** (permission.repository.ts):
   - ✗ mapPermissionResponse with valid response
   - ✗ mapPermissionResponse with null data
   - ✗ mapPermissionResponse with missing invoiceCashbackRedemption

3. **Service Layer Tests** (permission.service.ts):
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed - success path (redeemedAtDate exists)
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed - no redemption path (redeemedAtDate null)
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed - API error response
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed - network error
   - ✗ markAsRedeemed - success path
   - ✗ markAsRedeemed - API error response
   - ✗ markAsRedeemed - network error
   - ✗ Correct state mutation via store actions
   - ✗ Date formatting (ISO string)

4. **Store Layer Tests** (permission.store.ts):
   - ✗ Initial state correctness
   - ✗ setStatus action
   - ✗ setHasRedeemed action (also sets SUCCESS status)
   - ✗ reset action
   - ✗ State immutability

5. **Hook Layer Tests** (usePermission.ts):
   - ✗ Initial state selection
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed caching (returns cached value)
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed forceRefresh behavior
   - ✗ checkHasRedeemed with undefined accountId (throws)
   - ✗ markAsRedeemed success
   - ✗ markAsRedeemed with undefined accountId (returns false)
   - ✗ isLoading/isError state derivation
   - ✗ Function reference stability (useCallback memoization)

---

### 7. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS TO TEST

**Clean Architecture Layers**:
- Each layer has single responsibility (API → Repository → Service → Store → Hook)
- Dependency injection via constructor
- State management via Zustand slice pattern
- Hook composition returns object with state + methods

**State Flow**:
- UI calls hook method → Service sets LOADING status → API call → Service updates state
- Error handling at each layer with fallback behavior
- Status transitions: IDLE → LOADING → SUCCESS/ERROR

**Edge Cases to Consider**:
- Null/undefined accountId handling
- Empty/missing API responses
- HTTP error statuses (not just network errors)
- State mutation during concurrent calls
- Cached value handling with forceRefresh flag

---

### 8. RECOMMENDED TEST FILE STRUCTURE

```
__tests__/services/permission/
├── api.test.ts           (getPermissionApi, savePermissionApi)
├── store.test.ts         (PermissionSlice, all actions)
├── usePermission.test.tsx (Hook, caching, error handling)
└── (optional) service.test.ts (checkHasRedeemed, markAsRedeemed)
```

**File paths for reference:**
- Source: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/`
- Tests: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/__tests__/services/permission/`

input.content:
# Implementation Plan: Fix AirlineMiles Feature, Analyze Permission Feature, Implement Tests & Harness

## Context

This plan addresses multiple tasks:
1. Fix airlineMiles feature to follow the project style guide
2. Analyze and fix permission feature following the same standards
3. Implement comprehensive tests for the permission feature
4. Implement Anthropic Harness system (progress.txt + features.json)

The project is a micro-frontend package that uses Zustand for state management (migrating from Redux). The `newArch` folder contains the new architecture following SOLID principles with layered structure: api → repository → services → store → hooks.

## Research Summary

### Anthropic Harness System
Based on [Anthropic's engineering article](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents):
- **progress.txt**: Session memory log tracking completed work across context windows
- **features.json**: Structured feature inventory with validation criteria and pass/fail flags
- Enables incremental progress across multiple agent sessions

### Style Guide Key Points (`.harness/docs/1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`)
**Repository Layer**: Must contain pure mapping functions only, NOT classes
- Transforms API responses to domain models
- No business logic or API calls
- Example: `export function mapPermissionResponse(response): DomainType { ... }`

**Service Layer**: Classes for business orchestration
- Can instantiate APIs or repositories
- Orchestrates business rules
- Updates Zustand store state

**API Layer**: HTTP request clients
- Simple request/response handling
- No domain logic

## Critical Issues Found

### AirlineMiles Feature Issues

1. **Repository Pattern Violation** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`)
   - ❌ Uses class with singleton instance
   - ❌ Instantiates API class
   - ❌ Just passes through to API without transformation
   - ✅ Should: Delete this repository entirely (no transformation needed) OR use pure function if mapping required

2. **Service Pattern Violation** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services/airlineMiles.service.ts`)
   - ❌ Instantiates repository unnecessarily
   - ✅ Should: Call API directly (no repository needed)

3. **Import Path Error** (`src/newArch/store/slices.ts:3`)
   - ❌ Imports from old path: `"../../services/airlineMiles/store"`
   - ✅ Should: Import from `'./rewardProgram'`

4. **Duplicate Export** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts:1-2`)
   - ❌ Exports `usePermission` twice
   - ✅ Should: Add `export * from './useAirlineMiles'`

### Permission Feature Status
- ✅ Follows correct patterns (pure repository function, service class)
- ❌ Missing all tests (api, repository, service, store, hook)

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Fix AirlineMiles Feature Structure

**Commit 1: Remove unnecessary airlineMiles repository**
- Delete `src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`
- Update `src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/index.ts` (remove airlineMiles export)
- Update `src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services/airlineMiles.service.ts`:
  ```typescript
  import { airlineMilesApi } from '../api';
  import type { AirlineMilesTokenParams, AirlineMilesTokenResponse } from '../models';

  export class AirlineMilesService {
    async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
      return airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);
    }
  }

  export const airlineMilesService = new AirlineMilesService();
  ```

**Commit 2: Fix import paths**
- Fix `src/newArch/store/slices.ts` line 3:
  ```typescript
  import { AirlineMilesSlice, createAirlineMilesSlice } from './rewardProgram';
  ```
- Fix `src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts`:
  ```typescript
  export * from './usePermission';
  export * from './useAirlineMiles';
  ```

# Implementation Plan: Fix AirlineMiles Feature, Analyze Permission Feature, Implement Tests & Harness

## Context

This plan addresses multiple tasks:
1. Fix airlineMiles feature to follow the project style guide
2. Analyze and fix permission feature following the same standards
3. Implement comprehensive tests for the permission feature
4. Implement Anthropic Harness system (progress.txt + features.json)

The project is a micro-frontend package that uses Zustand for state management (migrating from Redux). The `newArch` folder contains the new architecture following SOLID principles with layered structure: api → repository → services → store → hooks.

## Research Summary

### Anthropic Harness System
Based on [Anthropic's engineering article](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents):
- **progress.txt**: Session memory log tracking completed work across context windows
- **features.json**: Structured feature inventory with validation criteria and pass/fail flags
- Enables incremental progress across multiple agent sessions

### Style Guide Key Points (`.harness/docs/1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`)
**Repository Layer**: Must contain pure mapping functions only, NOT classes
- Transforms API responses to domain models
- No business logic or API calls
- Example: `export function mapPermissionResponse(response): DomainType { ... }`

**Service Layer**: Classes for business orchestration
- Can instantiate APIs or repositories
- Orchestrates business rules
- Updates Zustand store state

**API Layer**: HTTP request clients
- Simple request/response handling
- No domain logic

## Critical Issues Found

### AirlineMiles Feature Issues

1. **Repository Pattern Violation** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`)
   - ❌ Uses class with singleton instance
   - ❌ Instantiates API class
   - ❌ Just passes through to API without transformation
   - ✅ Should: Delete this repository entirely (no transformation needed) OR use pure function if mapping required

2. **Service Pattern Violation** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services/airlineMiles.service.ts`)
   - ❌ Instantiates repository unnecessarily
   - ✅ Should: Call API directly (no repository needed)

3. **Import Path Error** (`src/newArch/store/slices.ts:3`)
   - ❌ Imports from old path: `"../../services/airlineMiles/store"`
   - ✅ Should: Import from `'./rewardProgram'`

4. **Duplicate Export** (`src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts:1-2`)
   - ❌ Exports `usePermission` twice
   - ✅ Should: Add `export * from './useAirlineMiles'`

### Permission Feature Status
- ✅ Follows correct patterns (pure repository function, service class)
- ❌ Missing all tests (api, repository, service, store, hook)

  export class AirlineMilesService {
    async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
      return airlineMilesApi.getToken(params);
    }
  }

  export const airlineMilesService = new AirlineMilesService();
  ```

  export * from './useAirlineMiles';

## COMPREHENSIVE EXPLORATION REPORT: airlineMiles Feature in newArch

### 1. FILE STRUCTURE & MAPPING

**AirlineMiles Feature Files:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/models/airlineMiles.model.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/api/airlineMiles.api.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/repository/airlineMiles.repository.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/services/airlineMiles.service.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/store/airlineMiles.store.ts`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts`

**Test Files:**
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/api.test.ts`
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/store.test.ts`
- `__tests__/services/airlineMiles/useAirlineMiles.test.tsx`

---

### 2. INTERACTION FLOW & ARCHITECTURE

**Layered Architecture (Clean Architecture Pattern):**

```
API Layer (airlineMiles.api.ts)
  └─→ Makes HTTP calls via @semparar/mf-app-user's api client
  
Repository Layer (airlineMiles.repository.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesApi, proxies calls
  
Service Layer (airlineMiles.service.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesRepository, proxies calls
  
State Management (airlineMiles.store.ts)
  └─→ Zustand slice with actions for loading/error/redirect URL
  
Hook Layer (useAirlineMiles.ts)
  └─→ Orchestrates service calls and state updates via useRootStore
  
Screen Layer (AirlineMilesMarketplace/)
  └─→ Consumes useAirlineMiles hook, renders WebView
```

**Data Flow for Token Retrieval:**
1. Screen calls `useAirlineMiles()` hook
2. Hook calls `fetchAirlineMilesToken()` callback
3. Callback invokes `airlineMilesService.getToken(AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS)`
4. Service chain: Service → Repository → Api
5. Api makes GET to `/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=/mileage`
6. Response success (codigoRetorno === 0) triggers `setRedirectURL()`
7. Store updates state, screen renders WebViewScreen with URL

---

### 3. CODEBASE USAGE LOCATIONS

**Screen Implementation:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts` - Custom hook `useAirlineMilesMarketplace()`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/screens/AirlineMilesMarketplace/view.tsx` - React component rendering WebView

**Hook Usage:**
- Imported and used in `AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts`
- Direct screen-specific logic wrapper around the store hook

---

### 4. PATTERNS & CONSISTENCY ANALYSIS

**Consistent Patterns:**
✓ Service/Repository/Api layering matches `permission` and `parameter` features
✓ Zustand slice pattern with state + actions object structure
✓ Hook uses `useShallow` from zustand for selector optimization
✓ useCallback with empty dependencies for stable function references
✓ Error handling with boolean return values

**Inconsistencies & Issues Detected:**

**CRITICAL ISSUE #1: Hooks Index Export Bug**
```typescript
// src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/hooks/index.ts
export * from './usePermission';
export * from './usePermission';  // ← DUPLICATE EXPORT!
// Missing: export * from './useAirlineMiles';
```
- `useAirlineMiles` is NOT exported from the hooks index
- Tests work because they import directly from the file
- Usage in `AirlineMilesMarketplace/index.ts` imports directly: `from '../../newArch/store/rewardProgram'` (works via rewardProgram/index.ts re-export)
- **This should be fixed to:** `export * from './useAirlineMiles';` and remove duplicate usePermission

**ISSUE #2: Store Cleanup Missing airlineMiles**
```typescript
// src/newArch/store/utils/cleanup.ts
export function cardPlaceStoreCleanup() {
  const {
    parameterPointsProgramActions,
    additionalCardActions,
    rewardPermissionActions,
    // ← MISSING: airlineMilesActions
  } = useRootStore.getState();
  
  parameterPointsProgramActions.reset();
  additionalCardActions.reset();
  rewardPermissionActions.reset();
  // ← MISSING: airlineMilesActions.reset();
}
```
- When store cleanup happens, airlineMiles state persists
- Could cause stale redirect URLs across different sessions

**ISSUE #3: Architectural Bypass - Thin Layers**
```typescript
// repository just wraps api
async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
  return this.api.getToken(params);  // Pure passthrough
}

// service just wraps repository
async getToken(params: AirlineMilesTokenParams): Promise<AirlineMilesTokenResponse> {
  return this.repository.getToken(params);  // Pure passthrough
}
```
- No business logic in Repository or Service layers
- Contrast: `permission.service.ts` has actual logic (checkHasRedeemed, markAsRedeemed)
- For simplicity, could flatten to just Api → Hook, but inconsistent with permission pattern

**ISSUE #4: Parameter Feature Has NO Hook**
```typescript
// Only permission and airlineMiles have hooks in rewardProgram
// Parameter uses service functions directly imported in permission.service.ts
```
- Inconsistent: parameter uses functional service exports, while airlineMiles/permission use hooks

### 2. INTERACTION FLOW & ARCHITECTURE

**Layered Architecture (Clean Architecture Pattern):**

```
API Layer (airlineMiles.api.ts)
  └─→ Makes HTTP calls via @semparar/mf-app-user's api client
  
Repository Layer (airlineMiles.repository.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesApi, proxies calls
  
Service Layer (airlineMiles.service.ts)
  └─→ Instantiates AirlineMilesRepository, proxies calls
  
State Management (airlineMiles.store.ts)
  └─→ Zustand slice with actions for loading/error/redirect URL
  
Hook Layer (useAirlineMiles.ts)
  └─→ Orchestrates service calls and state updates via useRootStore
  
Screen Layer (AirlineMilesMarketplace/)
  └─→ Consumes useAirlineMiles hook, renders WebView
```

**Data Flow for Token Retrieval:**
1. Screen calls `useAirlineMiles()` hook
2. Hook calls `fetchAirlineMilesToken()` callback
3. Callback invokes `airlineMilesService.getToken(AIRLINE_MILES_PARAMS)`
4. Service chain: Service → Repository → Api
5. Api makes GET to `/marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=/mileage`
6. Response success (codigoRetorno === 0) triggers `setRedirectURL()`
7. Store updates state, screen renders WebViewScreen with URL
